title: Goodenia heppleana
intro: Goodenia heppleana, also known as the hepple Goodenia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, and its leaves and stems are valued for their potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Goodenia heppleana include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
taxonomy: Goodenia heppleana is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Western Australia. Goodenia heppleana is a member of the genus Goodenia and the order Asterales. This plant is considered to be a part of the infrageneric classification Goodenia subg. Sutella. Goodenia heppleana is also a part of the larger classification of the family Goodeniaceae within the angiosperm clade.
